Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-14_18-55-19_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Failing test: "should oscillate a horizontal blinker into a vertical blinker"
Expected: nextGeneration([(0,0),(0,1),(0,2)]) returns [(-1,1),(0,1),(1,1)]
Current error: Expected length 3, received length 1
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-14_18-55-19_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/game-of-life.ts

Need to implement the reproduction rule: a dead cell with exactly 3 live neighbors becomes alive. The current implementation only handles survival. Use the neighbor count map approach — for every cell that gets counted (whether live or dead), apply the rules: alive if (currently alive and 2-3 neighbors) OR (currently dead and exactly 3 neighbors).

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the Green phase by adding the reproduction rule to the existing implementation.
I'll acknowledge the available skills. None of them are relevant to this minimal TDD Green phase task — I'll proceed with the implementation.

content.text:
All tests pass. 5 passed, 3 todo remain.

🟢 Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added the reproduction rule by extending the loop in `nextGeneration` with an `else if (!isAlive && count === 3)` branch that pushes the dead cell into the result when it has exactly 3 live neighbors.
**Result**: All tests now pass (5 passing, 3 todo)
**Approach**: Minimal addition — only what the blinker test demands. The existing neighbor-count map already tracks dead cells (since neighbors of live cells get counted regardless of liveness), so the only missing logic was applying the reproduction rule to the dead branch. No refactoring of structure, no premature generalization.

File: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-14_18-55-19_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/game-of-life.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
